page.primary_keyword: marbella hen stag weekend yacht cost
page.title: Marbella Hen and Stag Weekend Yacht Cost: Budget Breakdown

data.meta_description: Marbella hen stag weekend yacht cost: €749 for 2 hours on a 12.5m Astondoa. Full budget breakdown with drinks, skipper & taxes included.
data.summary: Complete budget breakdown for a marbella hen stag weekend yacht cost, covering our fleet from €749/2h to €4,719/4h, with all inclusions and local tips.
key_facts: Every charter includes skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and 21% VAT
All boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella
Maximum capacity ranges from 5 to 12 guests
No hidden extras – what you see is what you pay
Booking via WhatsApp for custom durations and boats

faq.q: What is the cheapest yacht for a hen or stag weekend in Marbella?
faq.a: The cheapest option is the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) at €749 for 2 hours. That includes a skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, snacks, insurance, and Spanish VAT. Perfect for a short celebratory cruise from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Are drinks included in the marbella hen stag weekend yacht cost?
faq.a: Yes, every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava as standard. You are welcome to bring your own additional drinks, but the basic bar is already covered in the price. No extra charges for ice or mixers.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and decorations on board?
faq.a: Absolutely. You are free to bring your own food, cakes, balloons, or hen/stag decorations. Just let us know in advance so we can ensure enough fridge space. The boat comes with a cooler and basic snacks are already provided.

faq.q: What is the maximum group size for a yacht charter?
faq.a: Our largest yachts (Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, Ferretti 94, Maiora 26m) take up to 12 passengers. For groups of 13 or more, we can arrange multiple boats departing together. The smallest boat (Dubhe, 8m) takes 5 guests.

faq.q: How far in advance should we book for a hen or stag weekend?
faq.a: We recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks ahead, especially for weekends in June through September. Peak dates fill quickly. A 50% deposit secures your slot, with the balance due 7 days before departure.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on our booked day?
faq.a: The skipper will assess conditions on the day. If it is unsafe to sail, we reschedule at no cost or offer a full refund. Marbella enjoys 320 sunny days a year, so cancellations are rare. We never sail in rough seas.

faq.q: Can we request a specific route or stops along the coast?
faq.a: Yes, the itinerary is flexible. Popular stops include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or anchoring near the Golden Mile. The skipper will suggest a route based on wind, currents, and your preferences. No extra charge for customising the trip.

faq.q: Is there a deposit required, and how do we pay?
faq.a: A 50% deposit is required to confirm the booking. Payment is via bank transfer or card link. The remaining 50% is due 7 days before departure. All prices include Spanish VAT (21%), so no surprise taxes on the day.

data.body_html:
Planning a marbella hen stag weekend yacht cost can feel overwhelming with so many options. This breakdown covers everything you need to know: from the cheapest 2-hour cruise to a full-day luxury charter with a jet ski. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and Spanish VAT — no hidden fees.

Our Fleet and Starting Prices

We operate from Puerto Banús, Marbella's most famous marina. Every boat includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. Here are the two price tiers for hen and stag groups:

  • Tier A – €749/2h up to €2,299/8h: Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax). Ideal for smaller groups wanting a short, lively cruise.
  • Tier B – €4,719 minimum 4h: Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) — includes a free jet ski. Perfect for a larger party with water toys.

For other boats (Azimut 58 Flybridge, Ferretti 94, Maiora 26m, etc.), pricing is on request via WhatsApp. We tailor the duration and vessel to your group size.

What’s Included in Every Charter

Transparency matters. When you book through Boat Rental Marbella, you get:

  • Licensed English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the entire trip (typically 40–80 litres per hour depending on the boat)
  • Unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
  • Light snacks (chips, nuts, olives)
  • Full insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish VAT (21%) — no surprise taxes

No extra charges for ice, mixers, or cleaning. You are welcome to bring your own food, decorations, or a cake.

Budget Breakdown by Group Size

Here is how the marbella hen stag weekend yacht cost scales for different group sizes:

Group SizeBoatDurationTotal CostCost Per Person
9 paxAstondoa 402 hours€749€83
11 paxAzimut 394 hours€1,499€136
12 paxMangusta 804 hours€4,719€393
12 paxFerretti 94Full dayOn request—

For groups of 13 or more, we can arrange two boats departing simultaneously from Puerto Banús. Just ask via WhatsApp.

How to Save on Your Hen or Stag Weekend

Booking a weekday (Monday–Thursday) often gives more availability, though our prices are fixed regardless of the day. To keep costs low:

  • Choose a Tier A boat for a short 2-hour cruise — €83 per person for 9 guests.
  • Bring your own decorations and cake instead of buying on board.
  • Combine the yacht trip with a meal at a chiringuito on the Golden Mile — we can anchor near one.

For larger groups, the Mangusta 80 at €4,719 for 4 hours includes a jet ski, which replaces the need for separate water-sport bookings.

Popular Itineraries for Hen and Stag Groups

Our skippers know the best spots. Typical routes from Puerto Banús include:

  • West to Estepona: 30 minutes cruising, anchor near the old town, swim in calm waters.
  • East to Cabopino: 20 minutes, stop at the dunes, then back past the Marbella Marina skyline.
  • Anchoring at Cala del Faro: A quiet cove with clear water, ideal for photos and jumping off the boat.

You can also request a stop at Sotogrande or simply cruise along the coast with music. The skipper adapts to your vibe.

What to Bring on the Day

Pack light but smart. Essentials for a hen or stag weekend yacht trip:

  • Swimwear and towels (we provide beach towels on request)
  • Sunscreen (SPF 50+ recommended, even in May)
  • Portable speaker (the boat has Bluetooth, but a backup helps)
  • Extra drinks or a bottle of bubbly for toasts
  • Decorations, banners, or a cake — no corkage fee

All boats have a shaded area, a small fridge, and a bathroom. The skipper will help with anchoring and music.

Booking Process and Payment

Ready to secure your date? Here is how it works:

  1. Send a WhatsApp message with your preferred date, number of guests, and desired boat.
  2. We confirm availability and send a 50% deposit invoice (bank transfer or card link).
  3. Balance due 7 days before departure.
  4. On the day, meet at Puerto Banús (we give exact berth details 24 hours prior).

For last-minute bookings (within 7 days), full payment is required. We accept euros, pounds, and dollars via transfer. Cancellations with 14+ days notice receive a full refund minus a 5% admin fee.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the cheapest yacht for a hen or stag weekend in Marbella?

The cheapest option is the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) at €749 for 2 hours. That includes a skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, snacks, insurance, and Spanish VAT. Perfect for a short celebratory cruise from Puerto Banús.

Are drinks included in the marbella hen stag weekend yacht cost?

Yes, every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava as standard. You are welcome to bring your own additional drinks, but the basic bar is already covered in the price. No extra charges for ice or mixers.

Can we bring our own food and decorations on board?

Absolutely. You are free to bring your own food, cakes, balloons, or hen/stag decorations. Just let us know in advance so we can ensure enough fridge space. The boat comes with a cooler and basic snacks are already provided.

What is the maximum group size for a yacht charter?

Our largest yachts (Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, Ferretti 94, Maiora 26m) take up to 12 passengers. For groups of 13 or more, we can arrange multiple boats departing together. The smallest boat (Dubhe, 8m) takes 5 guests.

How far in advance should we book for a hen or stag weekend?

We recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks ahead, especially for weekends in June through September. Peak dates fill quickly. A 50% deposit secures your slot, with the balance due 7 days before departure.

What happens if the weather is bad on our booked day?

The skipper will assess conditions on the day. If it is unsafe to sail, we reschedule at no cost or offer a full refund. Marbella enjoys 320 sunny days a year, so cancellations are rare. We never sail in rough seas.

Can we request a specific route or stops along the coast?

Yes, the itinerary is flexible. Popular stops include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or anchoring near the Golden Mile. The skipper will suggest a route based on wind, currents, and your preferences. No extra charge for customising the trip.

Is there a deposit required, and how do we pay?

A 50% deposit is required to confirm the booking. Payment is via bank transfer or card link. The remaining 50% is due 7 days before departure. All prices include Spanish VAT (21%), so no surprise taxes on the day.

Ready to plan your group trip? Browse our full fleet or send us a WhatsApp message for a custom quote. We’ll help you find the perfect boat for your hen or stag weekend in Marbella.
